name: Banger
classification_notes: Explicitly described as a blackletter by the foundry, loosely inspired by decorative blackletters and metal album lettering. Its spiky, high-contrast, display-oriented character places it firmly in the decorative blackletter subcategory.
summary: A single-weight display blackletter by Jamie Chang, built on spiky, curved letterforms drawn from decorative blackletter and metal album art traditions.
description: Released in 2025, Banger avoids straight lines throughout, giving every character a thorny, energetic quality that sits somewhere between traditional blackletter and heavy metal typography. At a single Black weight, it is a straightforward display choice for anything requiring visual aggression tempered by a degree of legibility. The glyph set is broad, covering over 300 languages across extended Latin, including full Vietnamese accent support.
foundry_blurb: Manic Type describe Banger as a thorny, prickly, and potentially explosive blackletter loosely inspired by decorative blackletters and metal album lettering. Designed by Jamie Chang and released in 2025 as a single style, the typeface deliberately avoids straight lines and is positioned as a charming, friendly and readable display face suited to anything that needs a little extra bang.

name: Antic
classification_notes: A grotesque sans with top-heavy humanist proportions in the tradition of Antique Olive and FF Balance, combining classic grotesque structure with subtle humanist stroke contrast.
summary: A 54-style grotesque superfamily by Jamie Chang drawing on Antique Olive, FF Balance and Duplicate Sans, spanning six widths and nine weights with a subtle top-heavy humanist feel.
description: Built from the bones of Manic Type's Picholine Antique, Antic takes the sleek, slightly eccentric proportions of Antique Olive and reframes them within a contemporary grotesque structure. The family runs across six width variants from Compressed to Expanded, each in nine weights from Thin to Black, totalling 54 styles. OpenType features include oldstyle figures, case-sensitive forms, fractions, square dots and multiple alternate character sets including humanist single-story forms.
foundry_blurb: Manic Type describe Antic as a delightfully expressive grotesque superfamily influenced by Antique Olive, FF Balance and Duplicate Sans. Built from the bones of Picholine Antique, it combines the sleek coolness of Antique Olive with classic grotesque features, offering a contemporary spin on the top-heavy humanist grotesque genre.

name: AdGothic
classification_notes: A bold, high x-height grotesque sans derived from Advertisers Gothic, a classic American advertising typeface from 1917, with condensed and compressed widths suited to display and headline use.
summary: A contemporary revival of Robert Wiebking's 1917 Advertisers Gothic, extended across four widths with OpenType alternates and variable font support.
description: Jamie Chang's interpretation of Advertisers Gothic preserves the bold, high x-height character of the original while adding practical width variants from Regular through to Compressed. Stylistic sets offer alternate forms for the tittle, A, e, t and hmn letterforms, giving designers meaningful control over the typeface's personality. A variable font is available on request, making it flexible for motion and screen applications.
foundry_blurb: Manic Type describe AdGothic as a contemporary interpretation of Advertisers Gothic, designed by Robert Wiebking in 1917, retaining their favourite moments from the original while improving usability through four widths and multiple stylistic sets. The designer recounts discovering the source at Letterform Archive Online and deciding to use it as a study, leading to this 2023 release.

name: Tumba
classification_notes: Tumba is explicitly classified as blackletter by the foundry, drawing on both uncial and fraktur calligraphic traditions with opentype alternate sets for each influence.
summary: A blackletter face by Jamie Chang that fuses uncial and fraktur calligraphic traditions into a single contemporary family with eleven styles.
description: Released in 2022, it blends the rounded open letterforms of uncial script with the angular formality of fraktur, offering opentype alternate sets for each influence so designers can shift the character between the two. Seven weights from Light to Black Inline sit in the main display family, while Tumba Text provides a companion subfamily at smaller sizes. The glyph set covers 292 languages across Latin Extended.
foundry_blurb: Manic Type describe Tumba as a mishmash of uncial and fraktur calligraphy influences, combining specific details from each tradition to produce a contemporary yet medieval typeface. They suggest it as suited to beer halls and bedtime story books, and note that the Text subfamily ensures there is a Tumba for every occasion.

name: Sudz
classification_notes: Sudz is a colour display script drawn from bubble letter and 90s graffiti traditions, using COLR font technology for layered letterforms. Its winding, exuberant strokes place it firmly in the display category with a graffiti script subcategory.
summary: A COLR colour font by Jamie Chang drawing on 90s graffiti and bubble letter traditions, built from winding layered strokes.
description: Released in 2024, this single-style display script uses COLR font technology to layer letterforms and tighten spacing in ways conventional fonts cannot achieve. Its forms reference 90s graffiti and bubble lettering, with alternates for C, G, S and r that shift between bubbly and cursive moods. Best suited to headlines, packaging and branding where exuberance is the point.
foundry_blurb: Manic Type describe Sudz as a script typeface built from winding strokes that create dynamic and vibrant forms, calling it a unique and fun display typeface made to excite and attract. It uses COLR font technology for letter layering and tighter spacing, and takes its inspiration from 90s graffiti and bubble letters.

name: Picholine Antique
classification_notes: Described by the foundry as a reimagining of Antique Olive as a British 'Antique', transforming its French sans-serif character into a slab-serif with generous proportions and warm personality.
summary: A slab-serif reworking of Antique Olive by Jamie Chang, giving the French sans-serif's distinctive proportions a warmer, British character with generous counters and true italics across seven weights.
description: Drawing directly from Roger Excoffon's Antique Olive, Jamie Chang has translated its unusual top-heavy contrast and compact forms into a slab-serif idiom, adding unbracketed serifs while preserving the original's generous x-height and condensed feel. The family runs across 14 styles with true italics and a broad OpenType feature set including small caps, oldstyle figures, single-storey alternates and case-sensitive forms. Cyrillic and Vietnamese support extend its reach well beyond Western European Latin.
foundry_blurb: Manic Type describe Picholine Antique as a reimagining of the classic Antique Olive as a British 'Antique', transforming the sleek French sans-serif into a generous and modern slab-serif. They characterise it as nostalgia-ridden, luminous and inviting, made for warm and humble voices.

name: Zeplin
classification_notes: A display typeface with variable axes controlling counter shape placement and weight distribution, drawing on Wild West lettering, brush calligraphy and Art Deco contrast. Its expressive, animated qualities place it firmly in the display category.
summary: A variable display font by Jamie Chang that shifts counter shapes across nine directional styles to produce unusual weight distributions suited to headlines and motion work.
description: Released in 2025, Zeplin uses two variable axes to move internal counter shapes in nine cardinal directions, named SouthWest through NorthEast, creating letterforms that range from conventional to deliberately lopsided. The approach borrows from Wild West wood type, brush lettering and Art Deco contrast, making it particularly suited to animated or motion-graphic contexts where the axes can be interpolated for effect. Case-sensitive forms, single-storey 'a' alternate and standard ligatures are included, with coverage across Latin Extended and Cyrillic scripts.
foundry_blurb: Manic Type describe Zeplin as a lively variable font designed to play with counter shapes and weight distribution, offering expressive possibilities including what they call delightfully weird animations. The design blends Wild West, brush lettering and Art Deco dramatic contrast, with styles named after cardinal directions referring to the location of internal letterforms within each glyph.
